title: Security Framework Change Dataset
description: Version records and dated milestone events for security-compliance frameworks that drive vendor questionnaires: releases, transition windows, phase schedules, and status changes, each with the official source behind it.
citation: Vorp Labs, Security Framework Change Tracker (data through 2026-07-15), https://vorplabs.com/security-framework-changes
coverageNotice: Version records and dated milestones for the frameworks this tracker reviews in depth, plus summary-tracked programs. It is not a census of every framework, and it is not legal or compliance advice. Licensed questionnaire content is not reproduced.

versionRecords.notes: v4.0.x accepted by STAR through December 2027; withdrawn January 2028.
CSA does not publicly state the v4.0.x question count on the transition pages; we do not repeat unverified counts.

versionRecords.artifactName: CSA AI Controls Matrix (AICM) with AI-CAIQ
versionRecords.structureFacts: 247 control objectives across 18 security domains; includes AI-CAIQ
versionRecords.notes: STAR for AI Level 1 self-assessment documented; further levels described as upcoming.

versionRecords.structureFacts: HECVAT 4 generation; adds privacy and AI questions relative to HECVAT 3.x
versionRecords.notes: Date reflects our latest page verification, not the 4.1.6 release date; per-release dates live in the Issue Tracker. Workbook content is copyrighted; version metadata only.

versionRecords.structureFacts: Annual content release cycle; licensed question content and current-cycle counts are not republished here.
versionRecords.notes: September 19 was the officially announced release date. The current product cycle and annual cadence are corroborated by Shared Assessments' public pages.

milestones.title: 32 CFR Part 170 CMMC program rule effective
milestones.whatChanged: The CMMC program rule took effect, defining levels, assessments, and the four-phase plan.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Defense-supplier questionnaires began referencing CMMC levels as a certifiable status rather than an aspiration.

milestones.title: Phase 1 begins, self-assessments in new solicitations
milestones.whatChanged: The 48 CFR acquisition rule took effect, starting Phase 1: Level 1 and Level 2 self-assessment requirements enter applicable solicitations.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Primes began asking subcontractors for SPRS scores and self-assessment affirmations in vendor questionnaires.
milestones.notes: 90 FR 43560. Phase 1 remains active as of July 2026.

milestones.title: Phase 2 scheduled start (superseded by the July 2026 suspension)
milestones.whatChanged: Phase 2, C3PAO third-party assessments for Level 2, was scheduled to begin one calendar year after Phase 1.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: This is the date most vendor content still cites; it no longer stands. See the July 13, 2026 suspension.
milestones.notes: Kept as a superseded record so the change history stays visible.

milestones.title: Phase 2 and later milestones suspended; 60-day program review begins
milestones.whatChanged: The Department of War suspended Phase II requirements and pending and future CMMC implementation milestones, effective immediately, while keeping Phase I self-assessment requirements in place. A 60-day study and a CMMC Reform Task Force were announced, with a public request for information.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Answer libraries citing a November 2026 C3PAO deadline need updating: third-party assessment timing is now unscheduled, while Phase 1 self-assessment attestations still apply.
milestones.notes: Administrative action; 32 CFR Part 170 text is unchanged and no Federal Register document had been published as of July 15, 2026.

milestones.title: 60-day CMMC review window closes (approximate)
milestones.whatChanged: The announced 60-day study of the CMMC program, counted from July 13, 2026, reaches its window; outcomes may restore, restructure, or replace the suspended phases.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Watch for a reinstated or redesigned assessment schedule; until then, treat Phase 2 timing as unscheduled.
milestones.notes: Approximate date derived from the announced 60-day study period.

milestones.title: Annex III high-risk obligations apply (moved from August 2026)
milestones.whatChanged: The 2026 omnibus agreement between the EU institutions moves Annex III high-risk obligations from August 2, 2026 to December 2, 2027; the change awaits formal Official Journal publication.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: AI governance questionnaire answers that cite the August 2026 high-risk date need the agreed December 2027 date, labeled as pending publication.
milestones.notes: Date status reflects institutional agreement, not yet formal publication.

milestones.title: CCM v4.1 and CAIQ v4.1 released
milestones.whatChanged: CCM v4.1: 207 controls across 17 domains, 11 new controls in the DCS, LOG, SEF, STA, and TVM domains and one IAM control removed. CAIQ v4.1 carries 283 questions aligned to the updated controls.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Answer libraries keyed to CAIQ v4.0.x row IDs need remapping to the v4.1 question set before the STAR transition closes.

milestones.title: STAR Registry accepts both v4.0 and v4.1 submissions
milestones.whatChanged: STAR Levels 1 and 2 began accepting submissions on either CCM/CAIQ v4.0.x or v4.1.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Vendors can time their v4.1 remap during the dual-acceptance window rather than at a hard cutover.
milestones.notes: CSA states month precision; day shown is the month start.

milestones.title: AI Controls Matrix v1.1 released with AI-CAIQ
milestones.whatChanged: AICM v1.1: 247 control objectives across 18 domains (up from 243 in v1.0), including the AI-CAIQ questionnaire and a STAR for AI Level 1 submission guide. CSA states mappings to ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST AI RMF 1.0, BSI AIC4, NIST AI 600-1, and the EU AI Act.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: The first standardized AI questionnaire package most vendors will receive; AI governance answers can now be structured against AI-CAIQ rows instead of ad-hoc customer questions.
milestones.notes: STAR for AI beyond Level 1 is described by CSA as upcoming, not live.

milestones.title: STAR acceptance of v4.0.x submissions ends
milestones.whatChanged: December 2027 is the last month the STAR Registry accepts CCM/CAIQ v4.0.x submissions; only v4.1 is accepted afterward.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Vendors maintaining STAR entries must complete the v4.1 remap before this window closes.
milestones.notes: Stated as month precision by CSA; day shown is the month end.

milestones.title: CCM and CAIQ v4.0.x withdrawn
milestones.whatChanged: CSA withdraws CCM v4.0.x and CAIQ v4.0.x: no further maintenance, archived only.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Any remaining answer-library rows keyed to v4.0.x reference a dead artifact after this point.
milestones.notes: Stated as month precision by CSA; day shown is the month end.

milestones.title: HECVAT 4 announced
milestones.whatChanged: EDUCAUSE announced HECVAT 4, adding questions on privacy and AI, more flexibility for institutional evaluation, and improved training materials, developed with Internet2 and REN-ISAC.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Vendors selling into higher education needed new privacy and AI answers; institutions gained updated evaluation guidance.
milestones.notes: Dated to the official announcement article; point-release history lives in the official Issue Tracker.

milestones.title: HECVAT 4.1.6 verified as the current version
milestones.whatChanged: EDUCAUSE lists 4.1.6 as the current HECVAT version; point-release deltas are recorded in the official Issue Tracker.
milestones.questionnaireImpact: Institutions typically request the current version; vendors keeping completed HECVATs on file should confirm which 4.x point release their answers target.
milestones.notes: Verification snapshot, not a release event; the official Issue Tracker carries point-release dates.
